description: Top-level rights/ tree (D4): join container for rights_record entries — the vendored schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json (EPR0-T2) plus its declared local amendment layer (EPR0-T3) is the shape authority for each entry in `records`. No `rights_record` may be inlined into any clinical KB JSON file (rules.json, candidates.json, evidence.json, reference-ranges.json); the only cross-reference between a clinical identifier and a record here is rights/rights-ledger.json. EPR0-T1 creates this container empty; EPR0-T4 seeds it with 6 triage-only records (one per KB-cited source from RF-EV-003), each at review.review_status: agent_triage_only and overall_status: UNKNOWN. EPR1-T1 (FR-WP1-01) adds a 7th, component-scoped triage-only record for modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json (the derived-fact channel that feeds deriveFacts() and all 91 rules, previously outside passage-level rights coverage), at the same review.review_status: agent_triage_only / overall_status: UNKNOWN posture. No agent-authored clearance status value may ever appear in this file (see schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's overall_status enum members prefixed with the forbidden clearance token for the exact list — that token string is intentionally not reproduced here).

change_triggers: an independently retrievable AAP artifact is located
review.notes: Triage-only seed (EPR0-T4, FR-WP0-04). No rights assessment has been performed; access basis and copyright status are unknown pending EP-R2 source-rights metadata work. Known, already-tracked context (not an assessed determination made by this record): RF-EV-002/REG-002 recorded HTTP 403/subscription-required for this source (see docs/audits/ep3-t5-passage-fidelity-audit-2026-07-20.md and evidence-packs/rf-ev-001/fidelity-findings.json finding EP3T5-F02); RF-EV-003 separately evaluated open-access substitutes for this source without altering this record's status (docs/project_plans/expansion/rf-handoff/rf-ev-003-oa-substitute-discovery.md).

change_triggers: a formal source-rights assessment is run for this source
review.notes: Triage-only seed (EPR0-T4, FR-WP0-04). No rights assessment has been performed; access basis and copyright status are unknown pending EP-R2 source-rights metadata work. RF-EV-003's discovery synthesis separately observed a WHO 2024 haemoglobin guideline in the open-access substitute landscape as CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO (docs/project_plans/expansion/rf-handoff/rf-ev-003-oa-substitute-discovery.md §4b) — that observation has not been verified against this specific KB source record and is recorded here only as unassessed context, not a determination.

third_party_components.component: Table 1 ("Normal Hematologic Parameters in Infants, Children, and Adolescents by Age and Sex, From Age 9 Months to 18 Years") underlying reference-interval data, credited by AAP itself to "(ref 42)" per the table title footnote (modules/anemia/evidence.json AAP2026_IDA#ev_002 sourceLocator.raw)
third_party_components.notes: AAP is a redistributor of this table's underlying data, not the originating rightsholder: AAP's own Table 1 title attributes the data to a numbered external reference ("ref 42") within the clinical report's bibliography. That reference has not been retrieved or independently identified, so it is recorded here as a distinct, currently-unassessed third-party rightsholder in addition to the American Academy of Pediatrics.

component_decisions.component_locator: modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json .ranges[] — 4 age bands ("6 to <24 months", "2 to <6 years", "6 to <12 years", "12 to <18 years") x 2 sexes (female/male) x 4 fields (hbLower, mcvLower, mcvUpper, rdwUpper) = 32 numeric values, re-expressed from AAP2026_IDA Table 1 (Pediatrics 2026;158(1):e2026077414); see modules/anemia/evidence.json AAP2026_IDA#ev_002 for the table-level source locator
component_decisions.notes: Triage-only (EPR1-T1, FR-WP1-01). No rights assessment of these 32 values has been performed; a formal component-level decision awaits EP-R2 source-rights metadata work and OQ-1 (decisions-block.md — whether reference-interval thresholds are measured/facts-strong or committee-judged).

change_triggers: EP-R2 source-rights metadata work formally assesses AAP2026_IDA access basis and copyright status
the AAP Table 1 "(ref 42)" third-party source is identified and independently assessed
OQ-1 (decisions-block.md) resolves whether reference-interval thresholds are measured/facts-strong or committee-judged
review.notes: Triage-only record (EPR1-T1, FR-WP1-01). Names the derived-fact channel explicitly: modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json -> deriveFacts() (modules/anemia/facts.anemia.js) -> all 91 rules in modules/anemia/rules.json. This channel sits outside the passage-level gating that covers the 32 AAP-citing rules gated via modules/anemia/evidence.json (findings §4, .claude/findings/rights-governance-spec-v1.0-review-findings.md — "the single most actionable technical finding in this review"). modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json also ships byte-identical to the browser SPA build (dist/modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json) — same content, two distribution surfaces (findings §4). No rights assessment has been performed; access basis and copyright status are unknown pending EP-R2. This record changes no value in reference-ranges.json and does not alter deriveFacts() behaviour (FR-WP1-04). record_scope: source_component — this record assesses reference-ranges.json as a component derived from the AAP2026_IDA source already tracked by RR-AAP2026_IDA; it does not replace or restate that source-level record.
